full_text:
More rain, less heat expected in Rajasthan this year:Heatwave days to drop; March saw above-average rainfall
- Copy link
The New Delhi Meteorological Centre has said that Rajasthan is unlikely to face severe heat this year, with both daytime temperatures and heatwave impact expected to remain lower than usual.
According to the forecast issued on Tuesday, March 31, night temperatures may stay higher than normal levels. Experts say this change is mainly due to an increased number of Western Disturbances and more rainfall activity.
They added that the effect of Western Disturbances was already seen in March, when intermittent rain and hailstorms over nearly 20 days helped keep the heat under control.
Relief from the heatwave this season too
This time, due to the day temperature remaining below normal, the number of heatwave days is also expected to be much less than normal. The areas of Jaipur, Ajmer, Bharatpur, and Kota divisions will experience the least heatwave.
Barmer, Jaisalmer, Jodhpur will experience heatwave effects for normal or slightly more days. Here, the daytime temperature reaches 47 to 49 degrees Celsius, but this year it will be lower.
When does a heatwave occur?
The Meteorological Department categorizes hot and dry winds blowing at temperatures 5 degrees above normal as a heatwave. Hot winds blowing when the maximum temperature crosses 47 degrees are considered a severe heatwave.
According to experts, the main reasons for heatwaves are global warming, lack of rain or clouds, and intense sunlight. The impact of heatwaves is most severe in plain areas.
Temperature in March reached close to 41 degrees, then fell
The first 10 days of March this year saw intense heat. After that, continuous Western Disturbances brought rain, storms, and hail, which controlled the temperature.
In other areas of Western Rajasthan including Barmer, Jaisalmer, Bikaner, Churu, Sri Ganganagar, the maximum temperature was recorded between 37 and 41 degrees Celsius. This March, the highest maximum temperature of 40.8 degrees Celsius was recorded in Barmer.
Due to the systems that arrived after March 11-12, the temperature gradually started to fall and came down to between 32 and 38 degrees Celsius, and by the end of March, the temperature remained below 40 degrees Celsius.
30 percent more rainfall in March this year
Due to continuous Western Disturbances, March this year also received more than normal rainfall. This season, from March 1 to March 30, rainfall was 30 percent more than normal. The average rainfall in the state in March is 4.2MM, whereas so far the average rainfall has been 5.4MM.
The highest rainfall occurred in Ajmer, Alwar, Bharatpur, Bhilwara, Bundi, Dausa, Dholpur, Jaipur, Jhunjhunu, Karauli, Kota, Rajsamand, Sawai Madhopur, Sikar, Sirohi, Tonk, Udaipur, Barmer, Churu, Jaisalmer, Jodhpur, Nagaur and Pali districts.
How much rainfall occurred in March in the last 5 years?
According to the Meteorological Center, from the year 2020 to 2025, the average rainfall in the month of March was 10 to 15 millimeters. Among these, the lowest was 8MM in 2021 and the highest was an average of 20MM recorded in 2023.
